steps.title: Contrast radio and TV results
steps.content: The first sentence says a radio-listener poll suggested Avery lost badly. The next sentence says the debate was ultimately considered a victory for him among television viewers. The blank must signal that reversal.

steps.title: Reject noncontrast transitions
steps.content: Choice A would restate the first idea, Choice B would show a result, and Choice C would show similarity. None fits a shift from apparent defeat in one poll to broader victory in another medium. The contrast transition is needed. The answer is D.
